attention2.ptxt: Applying more vacuum than –60 kPa (–0.6 kgf/cm2, –8.7 psi, –0.6 bar) will damage MAP sensor.

s1.ptxt: Check MAP sensor output voltage as follows. If faulty condition is found, replace MAP sensor.

s2.ptxt: Arrange 3 new 1.5 V batteries (1) in series, and check that total voltage is 4.5 to 5.0 V.
Connect voltmeter (2), special tool and batteries to MAP sensor as shown in figure.
Check that voltage drops gradually when vacuum is gradually applied with special tool.

attention3.ptxt: Environmental condition affects output voltage largely.
The sensor voltage below is reference value as measured under the following conditions.

item.ptxt: Atmospheric pressure: 101.3 kPa (1.01 kgf/cm2, 14.7 psi, 1.01 bar)

spec.title: MAP sensor reference voltage
spec.ptxt: Vacuum of –30 kPa (–0.3 kgf/cm2, –4.35 psi, –0.3 bar): Approx. 2.9 V
Vacuum of –60 kPa (–0.6 kgf/cm2, –8.70 psi, –0.6 bar): Approx. 1.7 V
